escalations.title: Mandatory ancillary benefits re-enrollment by 6/30 — never disclosed at signup

players.issueRole: Sent the open-enrollment notice; assigned benefit manager
Primary Vensure responder; confirmed the 6/30 lapse
Executive looped in for the requested leadership call
Service operations contact on the account
Raised the escalation on behalf of CrossPeak

escalations.summary: CrossPeak Solutions enrolled with Vensure in January 2026 under the understanding of a standard annual benefits term, with open enrollment at year-end. On June 12, 2026, Vensure announced a June 17–24 open-enrollment window with elections effective July 1, 2026. When CrossPeak questioned a mid-year enrollment, Vensure clarified that ancillary benefits run on a July 1–June 30 plan year (separate from major medical's January 1–December 30 year) and that ancillary coverage will LAPSE on June 30 if employees do not actively re-enroll. The July 1–June 30 ancillary cycle and the active re-enrollment requirement were never disclosed during sales or onboarding.
impact: Roughly two weeks' notice on a hard deadline: ancillary coverage lapses June 30 if enrollment is not completed.
Risk that employees lose ancillary coverage through no fault of their own.
Directly contradicts the expectation set at signup — a single annual term with year-end enrollment.
Described by CrossPeak as the latest in a recurring pattern of service failures with Vensure.

timeline.text: Announces 2026 Open Enrollment: portal open June 17–24; all employees must log in to elect or waive; elections effective July 1, 2026.
"Why am I receiving this?" — questions why an enrollment is being requested at all.
Clarifies the enrollment is for ancillaries, which run 7/1–6/30; major medical runs 1/1–12/30.

States no one with proper knowledge has explained this; was repeatedly told coverage was good for the year; expects year-end enrollment and asks that someone knowledgeable take charge and settle what must happen.
Confirms ancillaries will end 6/30 if no enrollment takes place; points to the benefit manager for plan questions.
Asks why this was not raised at signup; requests a call with Vensure's CEO and all parties; states this is one in a series of poor experiences.
Will connect with leadership to find available times. (Executive Richard Heckner added to the thread.)
Will compile a detailed, published record of every issue for review, and will review the signed agreements regarding the ancillary coverage term.

asks: Written confirmation of the exact ancillary benefits term CrossPeak agreed to at signup (e.g., a 12-month commitment from January 2026), citing the signed agreement.
An explanation of why the 7/1–6/30 ancillary plan year and the active re-enrollment requirement were not disclosed during sales or onboarding.
A call with Vensure leadership — CEO included — with all current parties on copy.
Confirmation of CrossPeak's options if enrollment is not completed by 6/30, and whether the lapse can be avoided given the late notice.
references: Email thread: "Action Requested: Open Enrollment Dates Available Now — CROSSPEAK SOLUTIONS — 45248" (June 12–17, 2026).
Vensure participants: Jadiel Aponte (Benefits Manager), Brittany Shively (AVP Service Operations), Brittany Taylor, Nate Schoenbrun, Richard Heckner (executive).
